perturbation.original_text: 3. The Parties hereto agree that information shall not be deemed Confidential Information, and Recipient shall have no obligation with respect to any such information which: (i) was generally known to the public prior to the disclosure under this Agreement; 1 <PAGE> (ii) is already known to Recipient prior to October 27, 1999, as evidenced by the written electronically stored records of the Party dated prior to October 27, 1999; or (iii) is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of Recipient or any person to whom the Recipient discloses such information; or (iv) is received by a third party without breach of this Agreement or any other obligation to maintain the confidentiality of such information; (v) is independently developed by Recipient; or (vi) is approved for release by written authorization of Owner; or (vii) is disclosed pursuant to the lawful requirement or request of a governmental agency, or disclosure is required by operation of law.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. The Parties hereto agree that information shall not be deemed Confidential Information, and Recipient shall have no obligation with respect to any such information which: (i) was generally known to the public prior to the disclosure under this Agreement; 1 <PAGE> (ii) is already known to Recipient prior to October 27, 1999, as evidenced by the written electronically stored records of the Party dated prior to October 27, 1999; or (iii) is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of Recipient or any person to whom the Recipient discloses such information; or (iv) is received by a third party without breach of this Agreement or any other obligation to maintain the confidentiality of such information; (v) is independently developed by Recipient; or (vi) is approved for release by written authorization of Owner; or (vii) is disclosed pursuant to the lawful requirement or request of a governmental agency, or disclosure is required by operation of law.  Notwithstanding the above, any information related to financial forecasts shall always be considered Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The added clause "Notwithstanding the above, any information related to financial forecasts shall always be considered Confidential Information" introduces a direct contradiction to the preceding list of exceptions. Specifically, item (i) states that information generally known to the public is not considered confidential. However, the new clause states that even if financial forecasts are publicly known, they are still considered confidential. This causes confusion about the status of financial forecasts that are already public.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. The Parties hereto agree that information shall not be deemed Confidential Information, and Recipient shall have no obligation with respect to any such information which: (i) was generally known to the public prior to the disclosure under this Agreement; 1 <PAGE> (ii) is already known to Recipient prior to October 27, 1999, as evidenced by the written electronically stored records of the Party dated prior to October 27, 1999; or (iii) is or becomes publicly known through no wrongful act of Recipient or any person to whom the Recipient discloses such information; or (iv) is received by a third party without breach of this Agreement or any other obligation to maintain the confidentiality of such information; (v) is independently developed by Recipient; or (vi) is approved for release by written authorization of Owner; or (vii) is disclosed pursuant to the lawful requirement or request of a governmental agency, or disclosure is required by operation of law.  Notwithstanding the above, any information related to financial forecasts shall always be considered Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause stating that 'any information related to financial forecasts shall always be considered Confidential Information'. This directly contradicts the original text, which lists exceptions to what is considered Confidential Information, including information that 'was generally known to the public'. If financial forecasts are publicly known, they would fall under exception (i), but the added clause overrides this, creating a strong contradiction. The contradicted text is located in the same section of the document as the Changed Text. This aligns with the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: 5. All written data delivered by Owner to the Recipient pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of Owner, and all such written data, and all copies thereof, shall be promptly returned to Owner upon written request, or destroyed at Owner's option.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. All written data delivered by Owner to the Recipient pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of Recipient, and all such written data, and all copies thereof, shall be promptly returned to Owner upon written request, or destroyed at Owner's option.
perturbation.explanation: The modification stating that written data delivered by the Owner remains the property of the 'Recipient', contradicts the first half of the sentence which states that it should remain the property of the owner. It creates confusion about ownership rights of the delivered written data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. All written data delivered by Owner to the Recipient pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of Recipient, and all such written data, and all copies thereof, shall be promptly returned to Owner upon written request, or destroyed at Owner's option.
perturbation.justification: The Changed Text states that 'All written data delivered by Owner to the Recipient pursuant to this Agreement shall be and remain the property of Recipient'. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, which states that the same data remains the property of the owner. This creates a direct conflict regarding ownership, leading to uncertainty and potentially unenforceable terms, fitting the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 1. One party hereto ("Owner") may disclose to the other party hereto ("Recipient") certain Confidential Information pursuant to this Agreement which Owner deems proprietary and confidential. The Parties agree that Recipient shall not use and shall prevent the disclosure of any information it receives from Owner that is marked PROPRIETARY AND CONFIDENTIAL, or similarly marked, or any other information (whether delivered in writing or verbally) which by its nature would be reasonably considered as confidential, to any other person, firm or corporation or delivered in connection with the evaluation of the Transaction, except as provided herein, and shall use the same degree of care to avoid disclosure of such information as Recipient employs with respect to its own Confidential Information of like importance.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. One party hereto ("Owner") may disclose to the other party hereto ("Recipient") certain Confidential Information pursuant to this Agreement which Owner deems proprietary and confidential. The Parties agree that Recipient may use the information received from Owner for its internal business purposes, even if it is marked PROPRIETARY AND CONFIDENTIAL. The Recipient shall use the same degree of care to avoid disclosure of such information as Recipient employs with respect to its own Confidential Information of like importance.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by stating that the Recipient 'may use the information received from Owner for its internal business purposes, even if it is marked PROPRIETARY AND CONFIDENTIAL'. This directly contradicts the original clause, which stated that Recipient 'shall not use and shall prevent the disclosure of any information it receives from Owner that is marked PROPRIETARY AND CONFIDENTIAL'. This creates ambiguity on whether the recipient can use the proprietary information or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. One party hereto ("Owner") may disclose to the other party hereto ("Recipient") certain Confidential Information pursuant to this Agreement which Owner deems proprietary and confidential. The Parties agree that Recipient may use the information received from Owner for its internal business purposes, even if it is marked PROPRIETARY AND CONFIDENTIAL. The Recipient shall use the same degree of care to avoid disclosure of such information as Recipient employs with respect to its own Confidential Information of like importance.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the Recipient 'may use the information received from Owner for its internal business purposes, even if it is marked PROPRIETARY AND CONFIDENTIAL.' This directly contradicts the original clause which stated that Recipient 'shall not use and shall prevent the disclosure of any information it receives from Owner that is marked PROPRIETARY AND CONFIDENTIAL'. Since the contradicted text is the same as the changed text, this creates a direct in-text contradiction. Therefore, the answer is YES, there is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Neither Party makes any representations or warranties as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information and neither Party shall have any liability to the other resulting from any use of the Confidential Information which is consistent with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. While both Parties warrant that all information provided is accurate and complete to the best of their knowledge, neither Party shall have any liability to the other resulting from any use of the Confidential Information which is consistent with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces a contradiction by stating "While both Parties warrant that all information provided is accurate and complete to the best of their knowledge", which directly contradicts the original clause "Neither Party makes any representations or warranties as to the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information". This creates a conflict regarding the level of assurance each party provides regarding the accuracy of the shared information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. While both Parties warrant that all information provided is accurate and complete to the best of their knowledge, neither Party shall have any liability to the other resulting from any use of the Confidential Information which is consistent with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that neither party makes warranties regarding the accuracy of confidential information. The changed text directly contradicts this by stating that both parties warrant the accuracy and completeness of the information. This creates a clear in-text contradiction because the two statements are mutually exclusive. The Contradicted text is the Changed Text itself.

perturbation.original_text: 13. Nothing in this Agreement shall impose any obligation upon the Parties hereto to consummate a Transaction or to enter into any discussion or negotiations with respect thereto.
perturbation.changed_text: 13. This Agreement shall impose an obligation upon the Parties hereto to negotiate in good faith to consummate a Transaction. If a Transaction is not agreed upon, the Parties may terminate negotiations after 30 days.
perturbation.explanation: The modification that "This Agreement shall impose an obligation upon the Parties hereto to negotiate in good faith to consummate a Transaction" directly contradicts the original text which states, "Nothing in this Agreement shall impose any obligation upon the Parties hereto to consummate a Transaction or to enter into any discussion or negotiations with respect thereto". This causes direct conflict on the obligation to negotiate the transaction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13. This Agreement shall impose an obligation upon the Parties hereto to negotiate in good faith to consummate a Transaction. If a Transaction is not agreed upon, the Parties may terminate negotiations after 30 days.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 13 imposes an obligation to negotiate in good faith, directly contradicting the original text which stated that there was no obligation to negotiate. This creates a clear and direct conflict regarding the parties' obligations under the agreement, as explained in the 'Perturbation Explanation'. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction exists.
